name: Greek Mythology
description: Gods, heroes, and monsters from ancient Greek legend

questions.q: Which Greek god is the ruler of the sea?

source.notes: Brother of Zeus and Hades. Wields a trident. Roman equivalent: Neptune.

questions.q: Who is the Greek goddess of wisdom and warfare?

source.notes: Patron of Athens. Born fully armed from Zeus's head. Associated with the owl.

questions.q: Which owl-like creature serves as the symbol of the goddess Athena?

source.notes: Little owl (Athene noctua). Represents wisdom and knowledge.

questions.q: Who is the Greek god of wine, theatrical revelry, and madness?

source.notes: Roman equivalent: Bacchus. Son of Zeus and mortal Semele.

questions.q: Aphrodite is the Greek goddess of what?

source.notes: Roman equivalent: Venus. Born from sea foam.

questions.q: Who is Zeus's wife, the queen of the gods?

source.notes: Goddess of marriage and family. Also Zeus's sister. Roman equivalent: Juno.

questions.q: Which Greek god is the blacksmith of the gods, master of fire and metalwork?

source.notes: Roman equivalent: Vulcan. Forged Achilles's armor and Zeus's thunderbolts.

questions.q: Persephone's annual return to the underworld is said to cause which season on Earth?

source.notes: Her mother Demeter (agriculture) mourns while she's with Hades, causing winter.

questions.q: Which mythological hero slew the Gorgon Medusa?

source.notes: Used a polished shield from Athena to avoid her petrifying gaze.

questions.q: Hades, ruler of the underworld, kidnapped which goddess to be his queen?

source.notes: Daughter of Zeus and Demeter. Eats pomegranate seeds, binding her to Hades part of the year.

questions.q: Which hero's only weakness was his heel, where his mother held him when dipping him in the River Styx?

source.notes: Greatest warrior of the Trojan War. Killed by Paris's arrow.

questions.q: How many 'Labors' did Heracles (Hercules) have to complete as penance?

source.notes: Including slaying the Nemean Lion and capturing Cerberus. Originally 10, increased to 12.

questions.q: Eros is the Greek god of love and desire. What is his Roman equivalent?

source.notes: Son of Aphrodite. Armed with bow and arrows (golden for love, lead for revulsion).

questions.q: Which Greek mythological creature had the body of a lion, the head of a human, and asked riddles?

source.notes: Oedipus solved her riddle about man (4 legs, 2, 3) at Thebes.

questions.q: Helios is the personification of which celestial object?

source.notes: Drove a chariot across the sky. Later identified with Apollo.

questions.q: Medusa was one of three sister monsters collectively known as what?

source.notes: The other Gorgons were Stheno and Euryale (both immortal). Medusa was the only mortal one.

questions.q: In the Odyssey, Odysseus famously blinded which one-eyed giant?

source.notes: Cyclops son of Poseidon. Odysseus identified himself as 'Nobody.'

questions.q: Atlas was condemned to hold up what on his shoulders for eternity?

source.notes: Commonly misremembered as 'the world.' The Titan holds up the sky/heavens.

questions.q: The River Styx serves as the boundary between Earth and which realm in Greek mythology?

source.notes: Dead souls were ferried across by Charon for a coin.

questions.q: King Midas was famously cursed with the ability to turn anything he touched into what?

source.notes: Granted as a wish by Dionysus. Backfired when he turned his daughter and food to gold.
